title: S-Comp verified-comparison contract
description: Specifies an exact numerical, finite, order, capacity, rank, or arithmetic comparison Phi whose conclusion is a C1-C5 certificate inequality or data for a declared residual payload. The record operationalizes the S-Comp comparison lemma schema in the structural exhaustion framework (branch_closure_methodology_extended.tex, "Lemma schemas").

schema.description: Required contract discriminator; it must be the literal `S-Comp`.

comparison.description: Required mathematical comparison to be verified. Typical framework instances include ledger closure comparisons sum w(d) >= l > sum kappa(p) (CT4 Charging schemes), budget-functional sign checks B_rho(Y) < 0, pigeonhole overload bounds (CT9), rank-budget comparisons (CT15 Rank forcing), aggregate mass bounds (CT14), finite-table exhaustion counts (CT10, C5), and well-founded termination comparisons (CT12 Peeling loop).

informal.description: Required nonempty prose statement naming both sides, their domains, every constant, inequality direction, and any `o(1)` or `o(n)` qualification.

latex.description: Optional LaTeX rendering of the exact equality, inequality, finite cardinality, order relation, or rank bound.

formalLanguage.description: Optional name and version of the language used for a formal comparison proof.

formal.description: Optional machine-checkable encoding of the same comparison.

comparisonKind.description: Required classification of the comparison: numerical for evaluated real/integer bounds, finite for exhaustive enumeration, order for minimality or well-founded relations, capacity for ledgers/budgets/charge maps, rank for rank quotients or forcing arguments, arithmetic for symbolic rearrangements, or other only when none of these accurately applies.

conclusionKind.description: Required role of the result: `certificate` when it terminates a branch, `payload` when it establishes data routed downstream, or `intermediate` when another proved step consumes the comparison.

conclusionRef.description: Required nonempty stable identifier of the certificate, payload, or intermediate proposition whose validity follows from this comparison.

assumptions.description: Optional list of all premises needed for the comparison. Include definitions of operands, sign/range conditions, residual membership, ledger assignments, payer capacities, budget currencies, slack-table entries, rank hypotheses, finite-table bounds, and previously proved comparisons used in substitutions.

items.description: One mathematical premise used by the comparison.

informal.description: Required nonempty prose statement of the premise, including its scope and numeric range.

latex.description: Optional LaTeX rendering of the premise.

formalLanguage.description: Optional name and version of the formal language used for this premise.

formal.description: Optional machine-checkable encoding of the same premise.

evidence.description: Required nonempty list of calculations, lemmas, tables, code, or formal artifacts that verify the comparison and its conclusion.

items.description: One evidence record supporting the comparison.

kind.description: Required evidence category from the fixed evidence-kind alphabet; use calculation for displayed algebra, code or table for a C5 finite verification, audit_record for worksheet/slack-table checks, and formal proof where the comparison is machine checked.

reference.description: Required nonempty locator, preferably a labelled framework result, S-Comp record, ledger or budget worksheet row, slack table, finite survivor table, verification theorem, audited code hash, or formal artifact.

hash.description: Optional integrity hash for code, generated enumerations, numeric outputs, or formal artifacts.

notes.description: Optional note recording substitutions, rounding direction, asymptotic threshold, or how the comparison yields `conclusionRef`.
